method: design-driven (single-pack proposal authored from operator-validated RnD outcomes; no raw GitHub issue intake)

proposals.title: MS Teams diarization cutover — replace caption-driven audio gating with pyannote segmentation + blue-square correlation
proposals.ceo_outcome: MS Teams transcripts no longer break when Teams' own captions lag, flicker, or drop. Speaker attribution is audio-driven, identity is correlated with the Teams DOM blue-square. Same architecture is reusable for Google Meet and Zoom in later packs.
proposals.cto_outcome: Replace caption-driven boundary control in services/vexa-bot/core/src/index.ts:1837 (the lastCaptionSpeakerId / flushSpeaker block) with diarizer-driven boundary control using pyannote/segmentation-3.0 + wespeaker, validated in pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd. Captions become an identity hint, not a flush trigger. No fallback path.
proposals.user_outcome: Dashboard transcripts switch speakers at the actual change moment (±50ms typical, vs ~1.5s caption lag). Whisper hallucinations on silent/short audio drop ≥80%. Brief interjections that Teams captions miss are correctly attributed.
out_of_scope: Same-gender 2-speaker corpus regression fix (needs segment embedding refinement pass; future pack)
Removing the RnD pack's services/vexa-bot/rnd/diarization/ sandbox
Replacing wespeaker (it stays as embedding + clustering model)
Speaker rename UI changes (reuse existing path)
proposals.release_id: TBD — bind to next numbered Vexa release at release-staging time
stitching_risks: Must land after RnD pack PR #379 is merged or tagged
Image size +32 MB from baked ONNX models — release hardenloop image-budget gate may need ack
No fallback path — diarizer load failure means bot fails to start (behaviour change)
Cross-pack dependency on SegmentPublisher.updateSpeakerName — currently shared with GMeet track-resolution
